findings.title: Hardcoded AWS access key in client-side upload widget
findings.evidence: raw diff line 8 adds `const AWS_ACCESS_KEY = "AKIAJW6PNQ4R7TLZ5KYM";` (AKIA-prefixed AWS access key ID) and line 16 passes it to `uploadDirect(event.target.files[0], AWS_ACCESS_KEY)`. The key is embedded in a JSX widget component that ships to the browser, exposing the credential to any client; Diff Drift flags this as High severity hardcoded secret.

notes: The diff imports a new `uploadDirect` helper from `./uploadDirect` that is not included in this packet, so its handling of the credential and upload target cannot be verified from the four files alone.
Even after this commit is reverted, the exposed key value `AKIAJW6PNQ4R7TLZ5KYM` should be treated as compromised and rotated, since it would have been committed to git history.
Diff Drift analyzed only `src/widgets/Uploader.jsx`; no files were marked as skipped/too large, and the single flag matches the concrete evidence in the raw diff.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
